title: Cylindrolobus validus
intro: Cylindrolobus validus is a plant species in the family Cucurbitaceae, commonly known as the 'Yellow Wood Sorrel', characterized by its yellow flowers and edible leaves. The health benefits of Cylindrolobus validus include its use as a diuretic, to treat kidney stones and urinary tract infections due to its flavonoid and tannin content. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made with Cylindrolobus validus include teas, infusions, and decoctions, often used in traditional medicine to aid in digestive issues and as a treatment for inflammation.
taxonomy: Cylindrolobus validus belongs to the genus Cylindrolobus, which is classified within the family Dicranaceae. The Dicranaceae family is a group of mosses, specifically belonging to the class Bryopsida. This classification places Cylindrolobus validus within the division Bryophyta, which is a group of non-vascular plants. The kingdom to which Cylindrolobus validus belongs is Plantae, which encompasses all types of plants. The kingdom Plantae is further divided into the subkingdom Viridaeplantae.
